metadata.title: DIY Trompe Passive Air Compressor System

public_links.title: Wikipedia: Trompe Historical Industrial & Mechanical Principles
OSMRE Applied Sciences: Passive Mine Drainage Treatment & Air-Lift Mixers using Trompes

technical_documents.description: Harnesses falling water to compress air without moving parts, powering pneumatic tools at zero ongoing energy cost using gravity-fed hydraulics.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Rural workshops in off-grid villages rely on expensive diesel compressors or hand pumps, limiting pneumatic tool adoption for construction and manufacturing.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: A trompe utilizes a hydraulic head drop (>1.5 m) to entrain air bubbles in a downpipe, separating compressed air in an underground collection chamber at 2–4 bar with zero moving parts.

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Identify a water source with at least 1.5-metre vertical drop and constant flow.
Step 2: Install the steel drum upright at the base, burying it 50 cm below ground for stability.
Step 3: Run the PVC downpipe from the water intake, sealing all joints with silicone.
Step 4: Fit a T-junction mid-pipe with an air intake snorkel extending above water level.
Step 5: Connect the air outlet valve to the chamber top, attach hose to workshop tool manifold.

comments.text: Installed this at our carpentry coop in Indonesia. Runs a nail gun all day on 2-metre head. Brilliant.
Has anyone tried this with less than 1m drop? Wondering if it still produces usable pressure.
